# Matriosha

Matriosha is an encrypted, auditable, model-agnostic Python CLI for local-first AI memory, managed sync, vault integrity, and accountable agent workflows.

Website: https://matriosha.in/
Primary documentation: https://matriosha.in/
Manual page: https://matriosha.in/matriosha.1
Agent setup guide JSON: https://matriosha.in/assets/matriosha_agent_setup_guide.json

## What Matriosha is

- A Python command-line interface for AI memory.
- Local-first by default.
- Encrypted at rest.
- Auditable through vault integrity checks.
- Model-agnostic.
- Designed for accountable AI agents and workflows.
- Supports managed mode with sync and agent tokens.

## Key concepts

- Local mode: memories stay on the user's machine.
- Managed mode: encrypted memories can sync to managed infrastructure.
- Vault: encrypted local storage with integrity verification.
- Memory commands: remember, recall, search, list, delete.
- Agent tokens: scoped access for automation and agent workflows.
- Autosync: managed-mode synchronization behavior.

## Suggested summary

Matriosha helps users and AI agents store, search, verify, and sync encrypted AI memory while preserving local-first ownership and auditability.

## Agent installation prompt

Matriosha publishes a machine-readable JSON setup guide for AI agents and coding assistants.

Use this file when an agent needs installation, bootstrap, command examples, or safe setup instructions for Matriosha:

https://matriosha.in/assets/matriosha_agent_setup_guide.json

## Important URLs

- Home: https://matriosha.in/
- Agent setup guide JSON: https://matriosha.in/assets/matriosha_agent_setup_guide.json
- Terms: https://matriosha.in/agb.html
- Privacy: https://matriosha.in/datenschutz.html
- Legal notice: https://matriosha.in/impressum.html
